nodes.notes: The invoke loop (shortcut -> overlay -> context -> prompt -> inject) must feel like native OS infrastructure, not an app. This is the Building Toward goal and the P0 demo moment.
Interview creates contexts with genuine insight — "What makes this *you*" must surprise the user. P0 demo moment #2.
All 7 FRs implemented, tests pass, Biome clean, tsc clean. The non-negotiable baseline.
Electron + React + TypeScript. No Tauri, no Swift. Fixed by PRD.
SQLite via better-sqlite3, WAL mode, no cloud. All data local.
OpenAI API only. gpt-4o-mini for interview + generation, gpt-4o for synthesis. No model choice.
Single-user, single-device. No authentication, no billing, no analytics.
pnpm for packages, Biome for lint, Vitest for tests, electron-builder for packaging.
OPENAI_API_KEY not yet available in environment. Must come from .env for dev or Keychain via onboarding at runtime. AI-dependent stories need this resolved.

Scaffold done: Electron main + overlay windows, React + Tailwind v4 + Framer Motion, Biome + Vitest + jsdom + testing-library, electron-builder configured, typed IPC bridge, design tokens defined. All infrastructure stories can proceed.
PRD orders phases by demo impact: scaffold -> Quick Invoke -> Interview+Synthesis -> Reveal+Onboarding. This ordering matches the Building Toward priority — invoke loop first.

agent-prompt.md exists with complete system prompts for all 3 AI steps (interview turn, synthesis, quick invoke generation). This is load-bearing infrastructure — the prompts define output quality. Stories that integrate AI should reference this file directly.

AppleScript paste simulation is the most fragile link. Will fail in Electron apps with custom paste handlers, locked fields, Terminal. The fallback toast must feel intentional, not broken. This is a risk to the invoke loop magic.

PRD specifies frameless, transparent, alwaysOnTop BrowserWindow. Electron can do this but needs careful config — no shadow artifacts, correct z-ordering, transparent background. Getting this wrong makes the product feel like a hack.

better-sqlite3 integrated with WAL mode, FK enforcement, idempotent migrations, 2 realistic seed contexts. Schema matches PRD data model exactly. The seed contexts are high-quality — they demonstrate what synthesis should produce, which sets a quality bar for the interview AI.
nodes.reason: From building: seed data quality matters because it's what users see in dev/demo before real interviews exist. The "Founder" and "Traveler" contexts model the output fidelity the synthesis engine must match.

nodes.notes: Merged lower-priority observations: obs:openai-client-ready, obs:tray-app-shell, obs:shortcut-registered
active
nodes.serves_building_toward: The Analysis identifies number-key selection as what separates "fast" from "magic." The keyboard-only path (Cmd+Shift+8 -> 1 -> prompt -> Enter) is the primary designed flow. Measuring and optimizing this path is directly the Building Toward goal.

nodes.notes: active
nodes.serves_building_toward: The Analysis identifies perceived latency as the silent killer. Streaming from token one feels faster than batch delivery. The streaming UX in GenerationPreview directly affects whether the invoke loop feels like magic or waiting.

nodes.notes: active
nodes.serves_building_toward: The Analysis names the biggest risk: gpt-4o-mini with a context block might produce text only marginally better than without. A side-by-side comparison during the build would validate or invalidate the core product thesis before demo day.

nodes.notes: active
nodes.serves_building_toward: The Analysis notes the PRD doesn't address what happens when a new user hits the shortcut with 0 contexts. This state is the first impression of the invoke loop for users who haven't finished onboarding. Guiding them to create a context preserves the magic rather than showing a dead panel.
